model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Arizona enters this Elite Eight matchup as the more balanced and analytically superior team, boasting a top-10 offense and the nation's 3rd-ranked adjusted defense. While Purdue possesses the country's most efficient offense, their 36th-ranked defense will likely struggle to contain a Wildcats squad that excels in transition and features multiple first-round NBA talents. Arizona's elite defensive length and superior overall efficiency margin should allow them to get the critical stops needed to neutralize Purdue's interior dominance and secure a trip to the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: West Regional Final: Arizona vs Purdue
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona's elite defensive profile and superior overall efficiency margin make them the favorites against Purdue's top-ranked offense in a battle of top-10 analytical powerhouses.
evidence: Arizona ranks 3rd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with a top-3 adjusted defensive efficiency.
Purdue boasts the #1 adjusted offensive efficiency but ranks 36th in adjusted defense.
Arizona holds a 31-2 record compared to Purdue's 26-8, reflecting greater consistency against high-level competition.
Advanced metrics show a significant gap in Adjusted Efficiency Margin (Arizona 37.56 vs Purdue 30.50).

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: e8-west-1
Current game label: West Regional Final
Round: Elite 8
Resolved teams: Arizona (arizona) vs Purdue (purdue)
Prior committed picks: 61
